title: GH #14: product_root config so bee reads product docs from a nested repo, not the workshop root
action: New `resolveProductRoot(root)` in state.mjs: reads `.bee/config.json` product_root (relative-to-bee-root or absolute). Unset/empty -> the bee root (FROZEN: zero change for every ordinary repo). Set+exists -> resolved product dir. Set+missing -> loud stderr warn (breaks #14's silent-null outage), returns the resolved missing path (reads find nothing, never crash). Wired the PRODUCT-side doc reads to it: backlog.mjs backlogPath (docs/backlog.md) + updateReadmeBadges (product README); inject.mjs projectMapLines (docs/specs count + reading-map); hooks/bee-session-close.mjs specs nudge (via libModuleUrl dynamic import). docs/history/* + .bee/* stay on the bee root (workshop side) — NOT redirected. Documented product_root in docs/config-reference.md.

truths: product_root unset -> resolveProductRoot returns the bee root (byte-identical to today; frozen by test).
product_root set to an existing dir -> backlog/specs reads resolve there.
product_root set but missing -> loud stderr warn naming product_root + #14, no crash.
docs/history/ and .bee/ are NOT routed through product_root (only product docs are).
artifacts: Freeze test (unset->root, 3 cases) + new-behavior test (relative/absolute/missing) + backlog integration test in test_lib.
Full 21-suite verify green after mirror sync + manifest regenerate; state.mjs export allowlist updated.
prohibitions: Do NOT change resolveRoots/findRepoRoot or any .bee-runtime path resolution.
No mirror left out of sync.

trace.outcome: product_root config redirects product-doc reads (backlog.md, docs/specs/, product README, session-close specs nudge) to a nested product repo; default unset = bee root, frozen zero-change; set-but-missing warns loud not silent
trace.verification_evidence:
Before (red/prior behavior — GitHub #14, confirmed live-broken on forgent for 3 days):
- bee.mjs resolves ONE root (findRepoRoot); backlog.mjs joined docs/backlog.md and inject.mjs joined docs/specs/ onto it. When .bee/ is in a workshop root but product docs live in a nested repo (./repo), those reads landed on the empty workshop docs/ and returned NULL SILENTLY: `bee backlog rank` -> "No parseable backlog table", `bee backlog counts` -> "No docs/backlog.md found", session preamble -> "Specced areas: 0" (even though 3 areas were specced in ./repo/docs/specs), repeatedly offering a redundant scribing bootstrap. No error surfaced for 3 days.
After: new resolveProductRoot(root) reads .bee/config.json product_root; product-doc reads route through it. With product_root="repo" the same calls now read ./repo/docs/*. Default (unset) = the bee root, byte-identical to before (frozen by test). A misconfigured product_root warns loudly to stderr instead of silent null.
Evidence: test_lib 337/0 — FREEZE test asserts resolveProductRoot(root)===root for no-config / no-key / empty-string (3 cases); new-behavior test asserts relative resolves under bee root, absolute honored, set-but-missing warns naming product_root+#14 and returns the resolved path; integration test proves readBacklogCounts(dir) is null without product_root and reads ./repo/docs/backlog.md (done=1) with it. Full 21-suite chain CHAIN_EXIT:0; test_lib_mirror byte-identical; state.mjs export allowlist updated. Scope frozen: resolveRoots/findRepoRoot and all .bee/* + docs/history/ paths untouched (only product docs redirected).
trace.verify_output: test_lib 337/0 incl the new resolveProductRoot freeze test (unset->root x3) + relative/absolute/missing cases + backlog integration (reads ./repo/docs/backlog.md when product_root=repo); test_lib_mirror byte-identical; release_manifest --check 141 match. Full 21-suite chain CHAIN_EXIT:0.
